title: Auto-bootstrap on first use
description:
# Auto-bootstrap on first use

## Background
The mandatory first-run sequence is init -> index -> scan. Agents forget it and
hit "run init first" errors; humans hit it on every new repo. This feature makes
the first interaction just work by lazily bootstrapping CodeScent's own state
when it is missing or clearly stale.

## Why this serves the product
Removes the single biggest first-run friction (intuitive, user-friendly,
ergonomic) while strictly honoring the read-only + no-network boundaries: it
writes ONLY to `.codescent/`, never to analyzed source, and performs no network
I/O. Pairs naturally with the capability guide.

## Design (authoritative decisions)
- A single `services/bootstrap.py` helper, invoked from the service entry path
  (and from start_task), checks `freshness`/`paths`. If `.codescent/` is missing
  or the index is clearly stale, run the minimal init -> index -> scan, bounded
  and logged, then proceed with the original call.
- Idempotent: present-and-fresh state is a no-op.
- Opt-out via config `auto_bootstrap = true|false` (default true). Document it.
- The triggering tool's payload carries a small `bootstrapped: true/false` note
  (+ what was run) so callers see it happened.

## Acceptance criteria
- First tool call against a repo with no `.codescent/` returns a useful answer
  with `bootstrapped: true`, having created only `.codescent/` state.
- Present + fresh -> `bootstrapped: false`, no redundant work.
- `auto_bootstrap = false` -> legacy behavior (clear "run init" guidance), no
  bootstrap.
- Source-read-only proof still passes (no analyzed-source writes, no network).

## Considerations / STOP
- STOP if any path would write outside `.codescent/` or require network.
- Keep bootstrap bounded; reuse existing repo_index/scan/freshness services.

## Subtasks
B.1 bootstrap service + unit tests; B.2 wire into entry path + config + note;
B.3 e2e + source-read-only proof.

title: Auto-bootstrap: bootstrap service + unit tests
description:
Parent: Auto-bootstrap on first use.

## Task
Create `services/bootstrap.py`: an idempotent helper that, given a repo, detects
missing/stale CodeScent state via existing `freshness`/`paths` and runs the
minimal init -> index -> scan, writing ONLY under `.codescent/`. Returns a
structured result (what ran, or no-op) for callers to surface.

## Acceptance criteria
- Missing `.codescent/` -> bootstraps; present+fresh -> no-op.
- Never writes analyzed source; never performs network I/O.
- Bounded and reuses existing index/scan/freshness services (no reimplementation).

## Tests (required)
- Unit: fresh temp repo -> bootstrap runs init/index/scan; assert `.codescent/`
  created and analyzed source untouched (git status of fixture clean).
- Unit: already-initialized fresh repo -> no-op result.
- Unit: stale index -> reindex path taken.
- Unit: assert no network (reuse the project's no-network test pattern).

title: Auto-bootstrap: wire entry path + config + payload note
description:
Parent: Auto-bootstrap on first use. Depends on B.1 (bootstrap service).

## Task
Invoke the bootstrap helper from the shared service entry path (and start_task)
so any first tool call triggers it when needed. Add a bounded `bootstrapped`
note to affected payloads. Add the `auto_bootstrap` config key (default true),
wire it through `services/config.py`, and document it in
`docs/configuration.md`.

## Acceptance criteria
- First call on a fresh repo succeeds end to end with `bootstrapped: true`.
- `auto_bootstrap = false` disables it and restores the explicit-init guidance.
- Config documented; defaults stated.

## Considerations
- Centralize the call so adapters stay thin; do not scatter bootstrap logic
  across individual tools.

## Tests (required)
- Unit/contract: payload note present and accurate; opt-out respected.
- Config round-trip test for `auto_bootstrap`.

title: Auto-bootstrap: e2e + source-read-only proof
description:
Parent: Auto-bootstrap on first use. Depends on B.2 (entry-path wiring).

## Task
Add an e2e script + integration test simulating a brand-new repo: delete/ignore
any `.codescent/`, issue a first MCP tool call, and assert it returns a useful
bounded answer with `bootstrapped: true`. Re-run the source-read-only proof
(scripts/prove_source_read_only.py style) to confirm no analyzed-source writes.
Detailed logging at each step.

## Acceptance criteria
- E2E proves first-call-just-works with detailed logs.
- Source-read-only proof passes after auto-bootstrap.
- Opt-out path exercised in the script.

title: Bus-factor / knowledge-silo signal
description:
# Bus-factor / knowledge-silo signal

## Background
Hotspot prioritization uses churn x size; co-change parses `git log`. Nothing
surfaces single-author concentration.

## Why this serves the product
A maintainability-risk signal that size/complexity miss: files that are
high-churn AND effectively single-author (knowledge silo / low bus factor).

## Design (authoritative)
- Reuse the single-pass `git log` idiom in `services/git` (NOT per-commit
  subprocess, per the prior audit). Compute author concentration + churn per file.
- Emit as a finding (`python/typescript.knowledge_silo`) or enrich the hotspot
  signal. Self-disable when no git history.

## Acceptance criteria
- High-churn single-author file -> silo finding; well-distributed file -> none.
  Deterministic; degrades cleanly without git history.

## Subtasks: .1 git author/churn analysis+tests; .2 finding rule+register+fixtures; .3 e2e.

title: bus-factor: implement + unit tests
description:
Parent: Bus-factor / knowledge-silo signal.
## Task
Add author-concentration + churn analysis to `services/git` using a single-pass
`git log` (reuse the co-change helper idiom; no per-commit subprocess). Output a
per-file concentration metric.
## Acceptance criteria: deterministic per-file author concentration + churn; no
network; degrades cleanly with no/short history.
## Tests (required): seeded git fixture -> expected concentration for a
single-author file vs a multi-author file.

title: bus-factor: register/wire + docs + contract
description:
Parent: Bus-factor / knowledge-silo signal. Depends on .1 (git analysis).
## Task
Emit the signal as a finding rule (`python/typescript.knowledge_silo`) or enrich
hotspot ranking; register it; add a git fixture; eval expectations; docs.
Self-disable when no git history is present.
## Acceptance criteria: rule_id in scan envelopes; eval updated; no-history -> no
finding.
## Tests (required): registration + deterministic eval update.

title: bus-factor: e2e/eval + logging
description:
Parent: Bus-factor / knowledge-silo signal. Depends on .2 (rule+register).
## Task
E2E on a git fixture: a single-author high-churn file -> silo finding; a
well-distributed file -> none. Deterministic. Detailed logging of concentration
values vs threshold.
## Acceptance criteria: correct flag/no-flag, stable, logged.

title: Self-describing capability & workflow guide
description:
# Self-describing capability & workflow guide

## Background
CodeScent exposes 40 MCP tools across 7 groups. Agents must currently know the
correct tool order from human-facing docs (docs/mcp-tools.md) they may never
read. Plan 015 (start_task) was created precisely because "starting a task
requires agents to chain many MCP calls and know the right tool order." This
feature gives agents a single, authoritative, in-band description of what
CodeScent can do and how to use it.

## Why this serves the product
Directly advances the core thesis: make agents disciplined and efficient. It is
the highest leverage-to-effort item in the 2026-06-28 idea set: trivial to
build, benefits every agent interaction, pure addition, zero behavior change.
Aligns with principles MCP-first, token-efficient, human-legible/agent-native,
and transparent/extensible.

## Design (authoritative decisions)
- The guide is GENERATED DYNAMICALLY from `core/public_surface.py`
  (`registered_mcp_tool_names()`, group metadata) plus the recommended workflow
  and the safety boundaries (read-only analyzed source, no network, bounded
  output). It MUST NOT hardcode a tool list, so it can never drift and adding
  future tools (e.g. resume_task, refactor_preflight) needs no edit here.
- Expose BOTH a `how_to_use` MCP tool and a `codescent://guide` MCP resource:
  some clients read resources, some only call tools.
- Output is one bounded payload: workflow steps, tools grouped by job with a
  one-line "reach for this when…", and safety boundaries. No source content.

## Acceptance criteria
- A single bounded payload returns the workflow + every registered tool grouped
  by job + safety boundaries, with no analyzed source.
- The tool set described equals `registered_mcp_tool_names()` exactly, computed
  at runtime (order-independent), proven by a contract test that fails if the
  guide and the registered surface ever diverge.

## Considerations / STOP conditions
- FIRST inspect `mcp/prompts.py`: if an equivalent guide prompt/resource already
  ships, FOLD INTO it instead of adding a parallel surface.
- Keep the payload bounded; if it grows large, summarize per-group rather than
  listing every field.

## Subtasks
A.1 render service + unit tests; A.2 register tool+resource+docs+contract;
A.3 e2e smoke with logging.

title: Capability guide: render service + unit tests
description:
Parent: Self-describing capability & workflow guide.

## Task
Create `services/guide.py` that renders a bounded guide object from
`core/public_surface.py` (registered tool names + their `group`), the
recommended workflow (init -> index -> scan -> pick finding -> context -> plan
-> tests -> verify -> mark), and the runtime safety boundaries. No analyzed
source content in the output.

## Acceptance criteria
- Pure, deterministic function/service: same surface in -> same guide out.
- Guide enumerates tools by group; the set of tool names equals
  `registered_mcp_tool_names()` computed at call time (NOT a hardcoded list).
- Output is bounded (cap per-group entries; summarize if oversized).

## Tests (required, in this subtask)
- Unit test asserting guide tool-name set == `registered_mcp_tool_names()`.
- Unit test asserting each tool carries a non-empty group + one-line purpose.
- Unit test asserting no analyzed-source paths/content leak into the payload.
- Unit test asserting boundedness (count caps respected).

## Notes
Keep business logic here, not in the MCP adapter. Reuse existing group metadata;
do not duplicate the public-surface registry.

title: Capability guide: register how_to_use tool + resource + docs + contract
description:
Parent: Self-describing capability & workflow guide. Depends on A.1 (guide service).

## Task
Register a thin `how_to_use` MCP tool AND a `codescent://guide` MCP resource that
serialize the `services/guide.py` payload. Update `core/public_surface.py`
(add `how_to_use` to the registered post-MVP set + PUBLIC_SURFACE tuple),
`docs/mcp-tools.md` (new tool reference + describe real payload fields), and add
contract coverage.

## Acceptance criteria
- `how_to_use` appears in `registered_mcp_tool_names()` and is callable over stdio.
- The resource and the tool return the same bounded payload.
- Contract test confirms registered surface == documented surface and that the
  guide's described tool set equals the registered set (catches drift in either
  direction).

## Considerations / STOP
- If `mcp/prompts.py` already exposes an equivalent guide, fold into it and only
  add what is missing; do not create a duplicate surface.
- Keep the adapter thin (serialize the service payload only).

## Tests (required)
- Contract test: surface/doc agreement + guide-vs-registered equality.
- Adapter test: stdio call returns bounded serialized payload.

title: Capability guide: e2e smoke with logging
description:
Parent: Self-describing capability & workflow guide. Depends on A.2 (tool+resource).

## Task
Add an end-to-end smoke (under `scripts/` and/or `tests/integration`) that boots
the MCP path, calls `how_to_use` (and reads the resource), and asserts the guide
lists every registered tool and all workflow steps. Include detailed step-by-step
logging so a human can read the smoke output and see exactly what was checked.

## Acceptance criteria
- E2E proves: guide tools == registered tools; workflow steps present; safety
  boundaries present; payload bounded; no analyzed source leaked.
- Verbose logging at each assertion (what was expected vs found).

## Tests (required)
- Integration test in the suite + a runnable script with logging mirroring the
  existing smoke/proof script style (see scripts/prove_source_read_only.py).

title: Characterization-test scaffolding
description:
# Characterization-test scaffolding

## Background
`suggest_tests` recommends tests; it does not emit runnable skeletons. Principles
8.6/8.7 push safe, verified refactoring — pinning current behavior first.

## Why this serves the product
Deepen `suggest_tests` to emit an honest, runnable characterization-test skeleton
(imports the target, calls it with placeholders, marks current-behavior assertions
as explicit TODOs) so an agent can pin behavior before refactoring.

## Design (authoritative)
- Skeleton generator service, Python first. Skeletons MUST be honest: they
  import/collect cleanly and never assert a fake green (assertions are TODO
  placeholders, clearly marked). Bounded; opt-in field on `suggest_tests`.

## Acceptance criteria
- For a target symbol, `suggest_tests` can return a skeleton that parses and is
  collected by pytest without producing false-positive passing assertions.

## Subtasks: .1 generator+validity tests; .2 wire suggest_tests+docs+contract; .3 e2e collect.

title: characterization-scaffold: implement + unit tests
description:
Parent: Characterization-test scaffolding.
## Task
Implement a Python characterization-skeleton generator: given a target symbol,
emit a test module that imports it, constructs/calls it with placeholders, and
includes clearly-marked TODO assertions (no fake-green). Bounded.
## Acceptance criteria
- Generated skeleton is syntactically valid and imports the target.
- No assertion silently passes (TODO placeholders, not `assert True`).
## Tests (required): unit asserting generated code parses (ast) + imports target.

title: characterization-scaffold: register/wire + docs + contract
description:
Parent: Characterization-test scaffolding. Depends on .1 (generator).
## Task
Add an optional `scaffold` field to the `suggest_tests` payload returning the
skeleton. Keep bounded. Update `docs/mcp-tools.md` + contract for the new field.
## Acceptance criteria: field documented; payload stays bounded; contract green.
## Tests (required): contract/adapter test for the new field.

title: characterization-scaffold: e2e/eval + logging
description:
Parent: Characterization-test scaffolding. Depends on .2 (wire suggest_tests).
## Task
E2E: for a fixture symbol, call `suggest_tests`, write the skeleton to a temp file,
and assert pytest COLLECTS it (no collection error) and it does not report a
false-positive pass. Detailed logging.
## Acceptance criteria: skeleton collected by pytest, no fake green, logged.

title: Execute CodeScent implementation plans 001-015
description:
Roadmap epic for expanding the implementation plans in plans/README.md and plans/001-015 into actionable beads. The overarching goals are to make CodeScent faster and safer as a local MCP-first code intelligence server, preserve source-read-only behavior for analyzed repos, keep public CLI/MCP contracts explicit, and improve agent workflows through bounded context, better ranking, deterministic findings, and evidence-backed verification.

Execution model: implement plans on focused branches, keep adapters thin, put behavior in services/engine/storage, update public_surface.py/docs/tests for public tool changes, update plans/README.md status rows when done, and never edit checked-in fixture repos except expected manifests explicitly called out by a plan.
design: Use plan-level parent beads for intent/scope/acceptance and step-level child beads for executable work. Sequential step dependencies make each plan runnable in order. Cross-plan blockers favor release-quality user outcomes over maximum parallelism: 002 follows 001; 004 follows 001 and 003; 005 follows 004; repo-wide scanner/workflow plans that would otherwise add new source reads or context orchestration (008, 009, 010, 011, 012, and therefore 015) wait for the bounded/index-backed context foundation through 005; 007 follows 006 to reuse the single-pass git-history idiom; 014 follows 013 to avoid concurrent SQLite schema-version collisions. The remaining early parallel lanes are foundation/context/search (001/003/004/005), git relationship ranking groundwork (006/007), and CI/storage ratchet groundwork (013/014).
acceptance_criteria: All 15 plan parent beads and their step subtasks are represented; hard dependencies from plans/README.md are encoded as blocking dependencies; soft/coordination dependencies are encoded as related links and comments; every implementation plan can be picked up from the bead text without rereading the original markdown.
notes: Source planning docs read: plans/README.md plus plans/001-context-related-files.md through plans/015-start-task-brief.md. Common verification baseline: uv run pytest; uv run ruff check .; uv run ruff format --check .; uv run basedpyright, plus plan-specific focused tests/evals. Revised after audit: ready queue intentionally starts with foundations that improve correctness, performance, bounded reads, git signal quality, and schema stability before additive scanners and user-facing workflow tools.
close_reason: Roadmap 001-015 complete: all plan parent beads and final sweep bead are closed. Final evidence: plans/README.md rows 001-015 DONE; contract/docs 59 passed; full baseline pytest 254 passed/1 Chrome-dependent skip, ruff check ., ruff format --check ., basedpyright; deterministic eval score 1.0; source-read-only proof ok; fixture source diff empty; schema migrations ordered through version 7.

comments.author: codex
comments.text:
Background and intent: CodeScent is a local, MCP-first server for codebase improvement. These beads preserve the plan authors' reasoning in the issue tracker so future agents can work from br ready/blocked queues without reconstructing context from chat. The first five plans harden existing context/search behavior and reduce source-read costs. Plans 006-015 add higher-leverage capabilities: co-change and hotspot ranking, configurable architecture boundaries, conservative dead-code and duplicate detection, coverage-backed test-gap findings, minimal test selection, health ratchets, evidence-gated resolution, and a one-shot task brief.

Guardrails: keep runtime analysis source-read-only; keep outputs bounded; do not introduce network access; keep MCP/Typer adapters thin; update public_surface.py, docs, and contract tests for every new public tool; coordinate storage schema version bumps; preserve checked-in fixture repos as intentionally flawed inputs.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Audit revision, 2026-06-15: the initial graph was complete and acyclic, but it exposed too many user-facing workflow tasks as immediately ready. I tightened dependencies where early implementation would likely create weaker product behavior or merge/schema conflicts. The goal is not to deny that some plans can technically work earlier; it is to make the br ready queue guide future agents toward the highest-quality implementation order for users.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Second audit revision: repo-wide scanner plans 008-011 and workflow plan 012 now wait for plan 005. Reason: these plans either parse/search source at repo scale or orchestrate context/search services. Implementing them before the shared bounded-read/index-backed foundation would create avoidable retrofit work and risk shipping new user-facing features on unsafe or slow hot paths.

title: Plan 001: Fix file-context related files
description:
Source plan: plans/001-context-related-files.md
Priority: P1
Effort: S
Risk: LOW/MED
Category: bug | context

Why this matters:
get_file_context currently exposes likely_tests and related_files, but related_files is effectively a duplicate of likely_tests. Agents requesting bounded context before opening a source file miss import, directory, search-similarity, and git-history relationships that get_related_files already knows how to compute. This is the first foundation stone because later context/index refactors need characterization tests around the public file-context payload.

Scope:
Touch context.py, context_support.py if helper extraction is needed, MCP context payload typing only if required, integration/contract tests, and the README status row. Do not change get_related_files result shape, add full source content, refactor persisted indexing, or edit fixture source.
design: Implement the child step beads in order. Each step carries its own acceptance criteria; the plan is complete only after the plan-level verification gates pass and plans/README.md status row for 001 is updated.
acceptance_criteria: Focused context and contract tests pass; ruff and basedpyright pass for touched files; get_file_context can return non-test related files when they exist; only in-scope files change; plans/README.md status for 001 is updated.
notes: STOP conditions: Stop if the current code no longer assigns related_files from tests, if the fix requires public MCP tool name or field removal, if a test requires editing checked-in fixtures, or if focused tests fail twice after reasonable fixes.
close_reason: Plan 001 complete; README status updated and focused verification passed

title: Plan 002: Reduce changed-source test false positives
description:
Source plan: plans/002-change-test-heuristic.md
Priority: P1
Effort: S/M
Risk: LOW
Category: tests | code-health

Why this matters:
python.changed_source_without_related_test should warn when changed source lacks obvious test coverage, but the current heuristic only checks tests/test_<stem>.py. That misses this repo's behavior-oriented layout under tests/integration, tests/contract, tests/security, and similar folders, creating noisy findings and weakening changed-file health signals.

Scope:
Touch code_health.py, search_queries.py only if a reusable helper is needed, focused scan/findings/search tests, deterministic expected manifest only if legitimately changed, and README status. Do not remove the rule, weaken assertions by deletion, change rule IDs/stable keys unless unavoidable, or edit checked-in fixtures.
design: Implement the child step beads in order. Each step carries its own acceptance criteria; the plan is complete only after the plan-level verification gates pass and plans/README.md status row for 002 is updated.
acceptance_criteria: False-positive regression passes; a no-test case still emits the rule; focused pytest and deterministic eval pass; ruff and basedpyright pass; only in-scope files change; README status row for 002 is updated.
notes: STOP conditions: Stop if reusing search_tests_for_repo creates an import cycle, if public finding IDs must change for unrelated rules, if eval failures are not clearly tied to this heuristic, or if fixture edits are needed.
close_reason: Plan 002 child beads complete, verification passed, README status updated

title: Plan 003: Batch multi-query content search
description:
Source plan: plans/003-batched-content-search.md
Priority: P1
Effort: M
Risk: MED
Category: perf | search

Why this matters:
multi_search_content is an agent-facing batching tool, but currently loops through queries and calls search_content for each one. That rebuilds inventory and scans every line for every query. It should scan files once, evaluate all queries during that pass, preserve bounded results, and keep ranking reasons/frecency behavior trustworthy.

Scope:
Touch search.py, search_support.py if needed, MCP search payload typing only if required, focused search integration/contract tests, and README status. Do not rename MCP tools or output fields, do not change single-query semantics except via shared helpers, do not implement persisted FTS here, and do not remove frecency recording.
design: Implement the child step beads in order. Each step carries its own acceptance criteria; the plan is complete only after the plan-level verification gates pass and plans/README.md status row for 003 is updated.
acceptance_criteria: multi_search_content scans inventory once per call, query:<query> reasons remain in merged results, focused pytest passes, ruff and basedpyright pass, only in-scope files change, README status row for 003 is updated.
notes: STOP conditions: Stop if preserving frecency requires a larger design decision, if MCP contract output must change, if one-pass batching requires storage schema/runtime state, or if focused tests fail twice after reasonable fixes.

title: Plan 004: Use the persisted index for context lookups
description:
Source plan: plans/004-index-backed-context.md
Priority: P1
Effort: L
Risk: MED
Category: perf | tech-debt | context

Why this matters:
CodeScent's product thesis is a local persisted index that serves fast bounded code intelligence. Several context paths still rebuild inventory and reparse source on every request even after RepoIndexService.index_repo persists files, symbols, references, and call edges. Moving hot context lookups onto SQLite reduces repo-size latency and aligns implementation with the architecture docs.

Scope:
Touch symbols.py, context.py, context_support.py, repo_index.py, schema.py only if indexes/migrations are required, context/repo_index/MCP contract tests, and README status. Do not rename MCP tools or fields, implement new FTS, add network/external parser services, change fixture source, or fold in source-read caps from plan 005.
design: Implement the child step beads in order. Each step carries its own acceptance criteria; the plan is complete only after the plan-level verification gates pass and plans/README.md status row for 004 is updated.
acceptance_criteria: find_symbol and get_symbol_context read persisted symbol data after indexing; get_file_context no longer extracts symbols across the full repo; plan 001 related-file behavior remains intact; focused pytest, deterministic eval, ruff, and basedpyright pass; only in-scope files change; README status row for 004 is updated.
notes: STOP conditions: Stop if persisted schema lacks data needed to preserve public payloads and fixing it needs broad redesign, if MCP fields must be renamed, if a fallback reparses the whole repo on the hot path, if external services/network are needed, or if focused tests fail twice.

title: Plan 005: Bound source-file input reads
description:
Source plan: plans/005-bounded-source-reads.md
Priority: P1
Effort: M/L
Risk: MED
Category: security | perf | bounds

Why this matters:
Public outputs are bounded, but several input paths still read entire supported source files into memory. One huge .py/.ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx file can stall indexing, search, context, or scanning even when the returned payload is tiny. A shared source-read budget makes the local daemon more resilient and makes the bounded-output promise honest.

Scope:
Touch inventory.py, context/ranges.py, Python and TS/React/Next rules, search.py, search_queries.py, a small shared helper under engine/core if needed, focused integration/contract/security tests, and README status. Do not add network/background workers/external services, change public tool names, return huge source content, edit fixtures, or implement persisted-index work from plan 004.
design: Implement the child step beads in order. Each step carries its own acceptance criteria; the plan is complete only after the plan-level verification gates pass and plans/README.md status row for 005 is updated.
acceptance_criteria: Oversized supported source files cannot force normal index/search/context/scan paths to materialize the entire file; normal fixture behavior remains unchanged; focused tests, deterministic eval, ruff, and basedpyright pass; only in-scope files change; README status row for 005 is updated.
notes: STOP conditions: Stop if a parser requires full-file reads with no bounded API, if public payloads would need huge content to stay compatible, if a cap introduces nondeterministic behavior, or if verification fails twice after reasonable fixes.

title: Plan 006: Add git logical co-change coupling to related files and impact
description:
Source plan: plans/006-git-cochange-coupling.md
Priority: P1
Effort: M
Risk: MED
Category: context | git | impact

Why this matters:
Files that repeatedly change together are often logically coupled even without imports. Adding co-change as a related-file/impact signal helps agents see workflow relationships that static structure misses, while keeping the implementation local and deterministic through a single git history pass.

Scope:
Touch services/git.py, context.py/context_support.py related-file scoring, focused context/git tests, and README status. Do not add public tool names, run multiple git subprocesses per helper call, invent a new scoring model, or edit fixture source.
design: Implement the child step beads in order. Each step carries its own acceptance criteria; the plan is complete only after the plan-level verification gates pass and plans/README.md status row for 006 is updated.
acceptance_criteria: git_co_change_counts exists and uses exactly one new git subprocess invocation; get_related_files can return a co_change reason; pytest, ruff, format check, and basedpyright pass; public_surface.py is unchanged; only in-scope files change; README status row for 006 is updated.
notes: STOP conditions: Stop if context_support has no per-reason weight mapping, if get_related_files no longer builds a reasons dict, if correctness requires more than one git subprocess, or if verification fails twice.

title: Plan 007: Rank improvements by hotspot score (churn x size)
description:
Source plan: plans/007-hotspot-prioritization.md
Priority: P1
Effort: M
Risk: MED
Category: findings | git | prioritization

Why this matters:
Not all findings deserve equal attention. A finding in a large file that changes often is more likely to slow agents and reviewers than the same finding in cold code. Ranking next_improvement by severity/rule and then churn x size turns CodeScent into a more practical advisor.

Scope:
Touch services/git.py and services/findings.py plus focused next_improvement tests and README status. Do not add MCP tools, alter finding IDs, run git in a per-finding loop, or edit fixtures.

title: Plan 008: Enforce architecture boundary constraints from config
description:
Source plan: plans/008-architecture-boundaries.md
Priority: P1
Effort: M
Risk: MED
Category: architecture | config | rules

Why this matters:
Teams need CodeScent to catch architecture drift, not just local code smells. Configured boundary rules let a repo express layer/package constraints and receive deterministic findings when imports cross forbidden boundaries, while staying opt-in so default scans remain cheap and unchanged.

Scope:
Touch core/models.py, create engine/rules/architecture.py, register an architecture pack in engine/packs.py, config loading/saving only as needed, tests, docs/configuration.md, and README status. Do not auto-fix imports, edit analyzed source, change existing rule IDs/finding shapes, run when no rules are configured, or touch fixtures.

notes: STOP conditions: Stop if config preservation would require a broad TOML rewrite, if import data is unavailable without large parser changes, if the rule cannot stay opt-in/zero-cost by default, or if verification fails twice.

title: Generic fallback language pack
description:
# Generic fallback language pack

## Background
Only Python + TS/React/Next packs exist. Repos in other languages are completely
blind to CodeScent.

## Why this serves the product
Cheap reach: a minimal, text-only fallback pack (file size, large-file, TODO
clusters, duplicate literals) for any language with no specific pack. Degrades
honestly — NO symbol/reference/structural claims it cannot prove.

## Design (authoritative)
- Register a lowest-precedence fallback pack in `engine/packs.py`; specific packs
  always win. Line/text heuristics only (no parser, no fake symbols).
- Config to enable/disable the fallback (default on). New rule_ids under a
  `generic.*` namespace (e.g. `generic.large_file`, `generic.todo_cluster`).

## Acceptance criteria
- A repo in an unsupported language yields only size/TODO/dup-literal findings,
  never structural/semantic claims; Python/TS repos are unaffected (specific
  packs still selected).

## Subtasks: .1 pack+text rules+tests; .2 register fallback+config+fixtures; .3 e2e.

title: fallback-pack: implement + unit tests
description:
Parent: Generic fallback language pack.
## Task
Implement a generic text-only pack + rules: file size / large-file, TODO cluster,
duplicate literal. No parsing, no symbol/reference output. `generic.*` rule_ids.
## Acceptance criteria
- Deterministic line/text findings only; no structural claims.
## Tests (required): unit per rule on a plain-text fixture; assert no symbol output.

title: fallback-pack: register/wire + docs + contract
description:
Parent: Generic fallback language pack. Depends on .1 (pack+rules).
## Task
Register the pack as lowest precedence in `engine/packs.py` so specific packs win;
add config to enable/disable (default on). Add a fixture tree in an unsupported
language. Document in `docs/language-packs.md` + `docs/configuration.md`.
## Acceptance criteria
- Specific packs still selected for Python/TS; fallback only used otherwise.
## Tests (required): precedence test (py/ts unaffected) + config toggle test.

title: fallback-pack: e2e/eval + logging
description:
Parent: Generic fallback language pack. Depends on .2 (register+config).
## Task
E2E: scan a repo in an unsupported language; assert only generic findings appear
(size/TODO/dup), no structural/semantic claims; assert disabling the fallback
yields no findings. Detailed logging.
## Acceptance criteria: fallback-only findings, toggle respected, logged.

title: Edit-stable finding identity
description:
# Edit-stable finding identity (content-anchored)

## Background
Plans 013-015 shipped the verification ledger, health ratchet, and calibration —
all keyed off finding identity. If finding IDs are derived from line numbers, they
break when code shifts, silently detaching ledger/ratchet history and corrupting
trust signals.

## Why this serves the product
Hardening that protects the persistent-improvement workflow just built. Robust +
reliable: findings keep their identity (and their verification/ratchet linkage)
across edits that move code around.

## Design (authoritative)
- AUDIT first: determine how `storage/repositories/findings.py` + the findings
  service derive identity today. If line-stable already, this becomes a
  characterization-test + docs task; if not, implement a content-anchored
  fingerprint (rule_id + normalized symbol/snippet + structural position) that is
  invariant to line shifts and insignificant reformatting.
- Migrate persisted findings; preserve ledger/ratchet/calibration linkage across
  the change.

## Schema coordination
- If a `SCHEMA_VERSION` bump is required, current version is 7; take the next free
  integer and DO NOT collide with the incremental-reindex bead (the other
  schema-touching item in this batch). `migrate()` applies in ascending order.

## Acceptance criteria
- A finding's identity survives edits that insert/remove lines above it; ledger
  and ratchet entries stay attached.

## Subtasks: .1 audit+characterization tests; .2 fingerprint+migration; .3 regression e2e.

title: finding-identity: implement + unit tests
description:
Parent: Edit-stable finding identity.
## Task
Audit current finding-id derivation in `storage/repositories/findings.py` and the
findings service. Document whether identity is line-stable. Write characterization
tests that capture today's behavior across a simulated edit (insert lines above an
existing finding, rescan) so the next subtask has a regression baseline.
## Acceptance criteria
- Written finding identifying exactly how IDs are derived today.
- Characterization tests pin current pre-change behavior.

title: finding-identity: register/wire + docs + contract
description:
Parent: Edit-stable finding identity. Depends on .1 (audit+characterization).
## Task
If not already stable, implement a content-anchored fingerprint invariant to line
shifts; migrate persisted findings and preserve ledger/ratchet/calibration links.
If a SCHEMA_VERSION bump is needed: current=7, take next free int, do not collide
with the incremental-reindex bead.
## Acceptance criteria
- New identity stable across line shifts; existing ledger/ratchet entries remap.
- Migration is ordered and collision-free; existing tests stay green.
## Tests (required)
- Unit: same logical finding -> same id before/after line shift.
- Migration test: pre-change findings + ledger survive migration.

title: finding-identity: e2e/eval + logging
description:
Parent: Edit-stable finding identity. Depends on .2 (fingerprint+migration).
## Task
Regression e2e: seed findings + a ledger entry + ratchet baseline, apply edits
that shift lines, rescan, and assert the logical finding keeps its identity and
the ledger/ratchet stay attached. Detailed logging of id-before vs id-after.
## Acceptance criteria
- Identity + linkage preserved across edits; logs show stability.

title: Go language pack
description:
# Go language pack

## Background
PRD Phase 8 candidate. The Python vertical (and the TS tree-sitter adapter) are
proven, so a third pack is now low-risk to add. Effort: L.

## Why this serves the product
Broadens CodeScent to Go repos with real structural intelligence (symbols, refs)
plus the core smells, using the same local, no-network, tree-sitter strategy as TS.

## Design (authoritative)
- Go parser adapter via tree-sitter-go, from installed local deps only, no runtime
  network, degrade gracefully when grammar confidence is low (mirror the TS
  adapter contract in `docs/language-packs.md`).
- Go rules: `go.large_file`, `go.large_function`, `go.missing_nearby_test`,
  `go.duplicate_literal` (+ size/complexity as feasible).

## Pack coordination
- Coordinate pack registration in `engine/packs.py` with the generic-fallback
  bead so precedence is well-defined (specific Go pack wins over fallback).

## Acceptance criteria
- A Go fixture yields correct symbols/references and the Go smell findings,
  deterministically and with no network.

## Subtasks: .1 Go parser/symbols+tests; .2 Go rules+pack register+fixtures+eval; .3 eval/e2e.

title: go-pack: implement + unit tests
description:
Parent: Go language pack.
## Task
Implement a Go parser adapter (tree-sitter-go) producing symbols + references from
installed local deps only; no runtime network; graceful degradation on low-confidence
grammar output. Mirror the TS adapter contract.
## Acceptance criteria: deterministic symbols/refs for a Go fixture; no network;
degrades cleanly.
## Tests (required): unit on a small Go file -> expected symbols/refs.

description:

expectations; document in `docs/language-packs.md`.
## Acceptance criteria: Go pack selected for `.go`; rule_ids in scan envelopes;
eval updated.
## Tests (required): pack-selection + registration + deterministic eval.

## Task

detailed logging of detected symbols/findings vs expected.
## Acceptance criteria: expected Go symbols + findings, stable, logged.

title: Import-cycle / dependency-SCC detection rule
description:
# Import-cycle / dependency-SCC detection rule

## Background
Import cycles are a top-tier, universally-agreed structural smell. The current
25-rule catalog does NOT cover them: `architecture.boundary_violation` checks
declared layer rules, not cycles. The import graph is already built during
inventory/reference indexing.

## Why this serves the product
A purely deterministic, evidence-rich new finding that fits the existing
rule/finding model exactly and gives agents a concrete high-leverage refactor
target. No tension with any product principle.

## Design (authoritative decisions)
- New rule module `engine/rules/import_cycles.py` runs SCC (Tarjan or Kosaraju)
  over the module import graph. Any SCC of size > 1 (or a re-export self-loop)
  is a finding. Evidence = the cycle path (ordered module list). Suggestion =
  the safest edge to break (e.g. move a shared symbol, invert a dependency).
- Rank findings by cycle size x churn (reuse hotspot churn signal).
- New rule_ids: `python.import_cycle`, `typescript.import_cycle`.
- Degrade gracefully on partial graphs from degraded parsing (report only
  high-confidence cycles; never crash).

## Acceptance criteria
- Deterministic detection of import cycles for Python and TS/JS with the cycle
  path as evidence and a safe break suggestion.
- No false positives on acyclic fixtures; bounded, ranked output.

## Considerations / STOP
- Reuse the existing import-graph data; do NOT add a second graph builder.
- If the TS graph confidence is too low for cycles, ship Python first and gate
  the TS rule behind confidence, documenting the limitation.

## Subtasks
E.1 SCC rule + unit tests; E.2 register rule + fixtures + eval expectations;
E.3 eval/e2e on cycle + acyclic fixtures.

title: import_cycle: SCC rule + unit tests
description:
Parent: Import-cycle / dependency-SCC detection rule.

## Task
Implement `engine/rules/import_cycles.py`: build/consume the existing import
graph and compute SCCs (Tarjan/Kosaraju). Emit findings for SCC size > 1 (and
re-export self-loops) with the ordered cycle path as evidence and a safe
break-the-cycle suggestion. Rank by cycle size x churn. Follow the existing
`engine/rules/model.py` finding shape.

## Acceptance criteria
- Deterministic; cycle path in evidence; safe-break suggestion present.
- Graceful on partial/empty graphs (no crash; only high-confidence cycles).

## Tests (required)
- Unit: a constructed 3-module cycle -> exactly one finding with the right path.
- Unit: acyclic graph -> no findings.
- Unit: self-loop via re-export -> finding.
- Unit: partial graph -> no crash, conservative output.

title: import_cycle: register rule + fixtures + eval expectations
description:
Parent: Import-cycle / dependency-SCC detection rule. Depends on E.1 (SCC rule).

## Task
Register the rule in `engine/rules/__init__.py` with rule_ids
`python.import_cycle` and `typescript.import_cycle`. Add checked-in fixtures
containing a KNOWN import cycle (and confirm an existing acyclic fixture stays
clean). Add eval expectations under `evals/fixtures`. Document the new rule_ids
in the configuration/rule catalog docs (and any thresholds).

## Acceptance criteria
- Rule discoverable in scans; rule_ids appear in scan envelopes.
- New fixture with a cycle is preserved as an intentionally-flawed input (do not
  "fix" it); eval manifest updated to expect the cycle finding.

## Considerations
- Per AGENTS.md: fixtures are intentionally flawed; preserve them. Update
  deterministic eval expectations so the suite stays green.

## Tests (required)
- Contract/registration test: rule_ids present in the public rule set.
- Deterministic eval expectation updated and passing.

title: import_cycle: eval/e2e on cycle + acyclic fixtures
description:
Parent: Import-cycle / dependency-SCC detection rule. Depends on E.2 (registration+fixtures).

## Task
E2E/eval run: scan the cycle fixture and assert exactly the expected
import-cycle finding(s) with the correct path; scan an acyclic fixture and assert
zero import-cycle findings. Deterministic across runs. Detailed logging of the
detected cycle paths vs expectations.

## Acceptance criteria
- Cycle fixture -> expected finding(s); acyclic fixture -> none.
- Deterministic (stable ordering); logs show detected vs expected paths.

title: Incremental dirty-file reindex + debounced watch
description:
# Incremental dirty-file reindex + debounced watch

## Background
No incremental path exists: every index run is full, and `watch`
(`cli/admin.py`) has no mtime/debounce/change-detection logic. On large repos
this is the main performance pain.

## Why this serves the product
Performant + reliable: reindex only changed files via mtime+content-hash, and make
`watch` debounce-driven. Correctness (cache invalidation) is the real risk, so the
e2e must prove incremental == full.

## Design (authoritative)
- Add per-file mtime+content-hash tracking in `services/repo_index` + `freshness`;
  compute changed/added/deleted sets. Reindex only the delta; handle deletes.
- `index` gains incremental-by-default with a `--full` escape hatch; `watch`
  debounces filesystem events and triggers incremental reindex.

## Schema coordination
- If persisting hashes needs a `SCHEMA_VERSION` bump, current is 7; take the next
  free integer and DO NOT collide with the finding-identity bead.

## Acceptance criteria
- Incremental reindex produces an index byte-for-byte equivalent to a full reindex
  for the same file set; only changed files are reprocessed.

## Subtasks: .1 change detection+tests; .2 wire index/watch+debounce+docs; .3 correctness/perf e2e.

title: incremental-reindex: implement + unit tests
description:
Parent: Incremental dirty-file reindex.
## Task
Implement change detection in `services/repo_index` + `freshness`: store per-file
mtime + content hash; compute changed/added/deleted sets vs the persisted index.
If a SCHEMA_VERSION bump is needed: current=7, next free int, coordinate with the
finding-identity bead.
## Acceptance criteria
- Correct change set across add/modify/delete/rename; deterministic.
## Tests (required)
- Unit: modify one file -> only it in the change set; delete -> removed; no change
  -> empty set.

title: incremental-reindex: register/wire + docs + contract
description:
Parent: Incremental dirty-file reindex. Depends on .1 (change detection).
## Task
Wire the incremental path into the `index` command (incremental by default,
`--full` to force) and into `watch` (debounce filesystem events -> incremental
reindex). Document in `docs/cli-reference` + `docs/configuration`.
## Acceptance criteria
- Incremental result equals full result for the same change set (proven in tests).
- `--full` still works; `watch` debounces and reindexes only the delta.
## Tests (required): incremental-equals-full on a changed set; watch debounce test.

title: incremental-reindex: e2e/eval + logging
description:
Parent: Incremental dirty-file reindex. Depends on .2 (wire index/watch).
## Task
Correctness + perf e2e: take a fixture repo, full-index it, mutate N files, run
incremental reindex, and assert the resulting index is equivalent to a fresh full
reindex (correctness) while only the changed files were reprocessed (log counts).
Detailed logging.
## Acceptance criteria: incremental == full; reprocessed-file count == changed
count; logged.

title: refactor_preflight — blast-radius bundle
description:
# refactor_preflight — one-call blast-radius bundle

## Background
The PRD names "make isolated edits without understanding blast radius" as a core
failure. Today an agent must chain get_impact + co-change + select_tests +
get_changed_file_health and know all four exist. This feature bundles them into
one bounded call.

## Why this serves the product
Embodies the safe-incremental-refactoring thesis as a single token-efficient
call. Low effort: pure composition of already-shipped services
(risk, git, context, coverage, verification). High perceived value, deterministic.

## Design (authoritative decisions)
- `services/refactor_preflight.py` composes existing services for a target
  file/symbol or a finding id: impact (callers/refs), git co-change coupling,
  minimal verification set (select_tests), and changed-file health. Dedupe and
  bound the combined payload; reuse each component's existing caps. Drop source
  ranges before raising caps if the payload is too large.
- Adds exactly one MCP tool name; land on its own branch.

## Acceptance criteria
- One call returns a bounded, deduped bundle: impact + co-change + tests +
  changed-file health, for a given file/symbol/finding.
- No new analysis is invented; results match the component tools called directly.

## Considerations / STOP
- Enforce the same per-section caps the component tools use; never exceed the
  most restrictive bound.

## Subtasks
D.1 preflight composition service + unit tests; D.2 register MCP tool + docs +
contract; D.3 e2e on a fixture with known callers/co-change/tests.

title: refactor_preflight: composition service + unit tests
description:
Parent: refactor_preflight.

## Task
Create `services/refactor_preflight.py` that, given a file/symbol or finding id,
composes the existing impact, git co-change, select_tests, and changed-file
health services into one bounded, deduped payload. No new analysis; orchestration
only.

## Acceptance criteria
- Output sections equal what each component service returns when called directly
  (proven by test), then deduped and bounded.
- Respects the most restrictive existing cap; drops source ranges before raising
  caps.

## Tests (required)
- Unit: for a fixture symbol, assert each section matches the component service's
  own output (composition fidelity).
- Unit: boundedness + dedupe (no duplicate files across sections beyond intent).
- Unit: missing inputs (e.g. no coverage data) degrade gracefully.

title: refactor_preflight: register MCP tool + docs + contract
description:
Parent: refactor_preflight. Depends on D.1 (composition service).

## Task
Register the `refactor_preflight` MCP tool in `mcp/planning_tools.py` (thin
adapter). Update `core/public_surface.py` and `docs/mcp-tools.md`; add contract
coverage for the real payload fields and bounds wording.

## Acceptance criteria
- `refactor_preflight` in `registered_mcp_tool_names()`, callable, bounded.
- Contract test: surface == docs; payload fields documented accurately.

## Tests (required)
- Contract + adapter test (stdio call returns bounded serialized bundle).

title: refactor_preflight: e2e on known-coupling fixture
description:
Parent: refactor_preflight. Depends on D.2 (tool registration).

## Task
E2E/integration on a fixture with known callers, git co-change coupling, and
nearby tests: call `refactor_preflight` for a target symbol and assert the single
response contains the expected impact, co-change, tests, and changed-file-health
sections, all bounded. Detailed logging.

## Acceptance criteria
- One call returns the full bundle matching independently-computed expectations.
- Bounded; logs show each section's contents vs expectations.

title: resume_task — post-compaction session brief
description:
# resume_task — post-compaction session brief

## Background
The PRD core thesis names "lose progress after context compaction" as a thing
agents are bad at. Plans 013-015 already persist the substrate: session events,
the verification ledger, and the health ratchet. This feature is the mirror of
start_task for CONTINUING work: it reconstructs "where was I and what's next"
from that existing state.

## Why this serves the product
High leverage, on-thesis, low effort because the data already exists. Turns
latent persisted state into a recovery superpower; compelling and deterministic.

## Design (authoritative decisions)
- A deterministic router (no new storage): read
  `storage/repositories/session_events`, findings, the verification ledger, and
  ratchet status; assemble ONE bounded brief: active/last finding(s), what's
  verified, ratchet status, recently touched files, recommended next tool call.
- Reuse `services/task_brief.py` patterns; consider extending it vs a new
  `services/session_resume.py` based on local cohesion.

## Acceptance criteria
- `resume_task` returns a bounded, useful brief reconstructable purely from
  persisted state (no analyzed-source dump).
- Recommends a concrete next tool call (e.g. get_finding_context / refactor
  steps) based on the last open finding + ledger.

## Considerations / STOP
- STOP/narrow if session_events lacks granularity to reconstruct a useful brief:
  then scope to "last N findings + ledger + ratchet" and document the limitation.
- Land on its own branch (adds exactly one MCP tool name; avoids contract-test
  merge churn).

## Subtasks
C.1 resume service + unit tests; C.2 register MCP tool + docs + contract;

title: Per-rule precision + health trend

# Per-rule precision + health trend

title: Subjective LLM review via MCP sampling (Phase 9)
description:
# Subjective LLM review via MCP sampling (Phase 9, no-network)

## Background
`services/subjective_review.py` is already scaffolded: a `SubjectiveReviewProvider`
Protocol, a `FakeSubjectiveReviewProvider`, a `SubjectiveReviewService` gated by
`config.privacy.allow_llm_review`, and `build_subjective_review_prompt()`. It has
no real provider and is NOT on the public surface. PRD Phase 9 wants opt-in
subjective review for things deterministic analysis cannot judge.

## Why this serves the product
Unlocks the whole Phase 9 while honoring Local-First / No-Network-by-default:
instead of the SERVER calling a remote API, it issues an MCP **sampling** request
so the *calling client's own LLM* does the judging. Subjective findings are
clearly labeled and never masquerade as deterministic facts (Phase 9 exit
criteria; principle 8.3).

## Design (authoritative)
- Add a `SamplingSubjectiveReviewProvider` that performs an MCP sampling request
  via the server session/context, using `build_subjective_review_prompt()`.
  Gated by `allow_llm_review` (default false). Degrade gracefully when the client
  does not support sampling (return a clear "sampling unavailable" result, no
  crash, no network).
- Expose an opt-in `subjective_review` MCP tool. Persist subjective findings with
  a distinct `subjective` provenance + confidence, merged into the backlog but
  visually/semantically separate from deterministic findings.
- Document the data-exposure implication (sampling sends code excerpts to the
  client's model) per PRD 14.5 Transparent Optional LLM Use.

## Acceptance criteria
- With `allow_llm_review=true` + a sampling-capable client, returns labeled
  subjective findings via the client model; no server network I/O.
- With it false (default) or no sampling support, clean no-op.

## STOP
- STOP if any path makes a direct network call from the server.

## Subtasks: .1 sampling provider+tests; .2 opt-in tool+config+docs+contract; .3 e2e.

title: subjective-sampling: implement + unit tests
description:
Parent: Subjective review via MCP sampling.
## Task
Add `SamplingSubjectiveReviewProvider` implementing the existing
`SubjectiveReviewProvider` Protocol: build the prompt via
`build_subjective_review_prompt()`, issue an MCP sampling request through the
server session/context, parse the result into `SubjectiveFinding`s. Honor
`allow_llm_review`; degrade gracefully (clear result, no crash) when the client
cannot sample. No direct server network I/O.
## Acceptance criteria
- Provider returns parsed subjective findings from a sampling channel.
- allow_llm_review=false -> provider not used. No-sampling client -> graceful.
## Tests (required)
- Unit with a fake sampling channel returning canned output -> parsed findings.
- Unit: gate off -> no-op. Unit: sampling unavailable -> graceful result.
- Unit asserting no socket/network use (reuse project no-network test pattern).

description:
Parent: Subjective review via MCP sampling. Depends on .1 (sampling provider).
## Task
Register an opt-in `subjective_review` MCP tool serializing the

`subjective` provenance + confidence (never as deterministic). Update
`core/public_surface.py`, `docs/mcp-tools.md` (Phase 9 wording: opt-in, subjective,
labeled, data-exposure note per PRD 14.5). Config `privacy.allow_llm_review`
default false, documented in `docs/configuration.md`. Contract coverage.
## Acceptance criteria
- Tool registered + documented; surface == docs (contract test).
- Subjective findings stored/returned with distinct provenance, never blended
  into deterministic counts.
## Tests (required)
- Contract test surface/docs agreement; provenance-separation test.

title: subjective-sampling: e2e/eval + logging
description:
Parent: Subjective review via MCP sampling. Depends on .2 (tool registration).
## Task
E2E: with `allow_llm_review=true` and a fake client model, run subjective review
end to end; assert findings carry subjective provenance + confidence and are
merged distinctly. With the flag false, assert clean no-op. Detailed logging.
## Acceptance criteria
- Opt-in path produces labeled subjective findings via the (fake) client model.
- Opt-out path is a no-op. No server network. Logs show each step + provenance.

title: Test-quality smells
description:
# Test-quality smells

## Background
The catalog has `missing_nearby_test` + coverage ingestion (does a test exist?)
but nothing on whether tests are worth anything.

## Why this serves the product
Deterministic, evidence-based new findings that raise real maintainability risk:
assertion-free tests, no-op/always-pass tests, over-mocking (high mock-to-assert
ratio), and skip/xfail clusters.

## Design (authoritative)
- New rules in `engine/rules` for Python and TS test files; detect test functions
  via existing pack/parser data. Evidence = the offending test location; suggestion
  = "add a meaningful assertion" / "reduce mocking" / "unskip or delete".
- New rule_ids `python.*` / `typescript.*` (e.g. `python.assertionless_test`,
  `python.skipped_test_cluster`). Bounded, ranked.

## Acceptance criteria
- Deterministic detection of assertion-free, no-op, over-mocked, and skip/xfail
  cluster tests; no false positives on healthy tests.

## Subtasks: .1 rules+tests; .2 register+fixtures+eval; .3 eval/e2e.

title: test-quality: implement + unit tests
description:
Parent: Test-quality smells.
## Task
Implement test-quality rules in `engine/rules` (Python + TS): assertion-free
tests, no-op/always-pass, over-mocking (mock-to-assert ratio), skip/xfail
clusters. Follow `engine/rules/model.py` finding shape; evidence + suggestion.
## Acceptance criteria
- Each smell detected deterministically with location evidence + safe suggestion.
## Tests (required)
- Unit per smell: positive fixture string -> finding; healthy test -> none.

title: test-quality: register/wire + docs + contract
description:
Parent: Test-quality smells. Depends on .1 (rules).
## Task
Register the rules in `engine/rules/__init__.py` with new rule_ids. Add checked-in
fixtures with known bad tests (preserve as intentionally flawed). Add eval
expectations under `evals/fixtures`; document rule_ids + any thresholds.
## Acceptance criteria
- rule_ids appear in scan envelopes; eval manifest updated and passing.
## Tests (required): registration test + deterministic eval update.

title: test-quality: e2e/eval + logging
description:
Parent: Test-quality smells. Depends on .2 (register+fixtures).
## Task
Eval/e2e: scan the bad-test fixture -> expected findings; scan a healthy-test
fixture -> none. Deterministic ordering. Detailed logging of detected vs expected.
## Acceptance criteria: bad fixture flagged, healthy fixture clean, stable, logged.

title: Top-5 integration & release-readiness sweep
description:
# Top-5 integration & release-readiness sweep

## Background
Mirrors the prior roadmap's cross-plan sweep (closed bead bg2.16). The top-5
features land independently, but three of them — capability guide (`how_to_use`),
`resume_task`, and `refactor_preflight` — each mutate `core/public_surface.py`,
`docs/mcp-tools.md`, and contract tests. Auto-bootstrap mutates config + payload
notes. This is the plan-space guard against local changes that pass in isolation
but leave the public surface, docs, or verification baseline inconsistent.

## Why this exists
Per AGENTS.md, public MCP changes must keep `public_surface.py`,
`docs/mcp-tools.md`/README, and contract tests in agreement. Run this AFTER all
five features' subtasks close to prove they compose into one coherent, bounded,
MCP-first, read-only, no-network workflow.

## Acceptance criteria
- `registered_mcp_tool_names()`, `docs/mcp-tools.md`, and contract tests agree
  on the full surface including `how_to_use`, `resume_task`, `refactor_preflight`.
- The capability guide enumerates the FINAL combined tool set (dynamic check
  passes regardless of merge order).
- Auto-bootstrap: `bootstrapped` note + `auto_bootstrap` opt-out documented and
  consistent across affected tools.
- Full verification baseline passes: `uv run pytest`, `uv run ruff check .`,
  `uv run ruff format --check .`, `uv run basedpyright`.
- Deterministic eval and source-read-only proof still pass.
- No checked-in fixture source modified EXCEPT the intentional new import-cycle
  fixture (which must remain unfixed, per AGENTS.md).
- New rule_ids `python.import_cycle` / `typescript.import_cycle` documented.

## STOP conditions
- Run only after A.3, B.3, C.3, D.3, E.3 are closed (this bead depends on them).
- If any surface/docs/contract disagreement is found, fix it here rather than
  shipping inconsistent state.
